text: When working with APIs, you send HTTP requests to a server and receive responses, often in JSON format. This lightweight data structure is easy to read and parse. Many APIs require authentication, typically using an API key, to control access. You also need to respect rate limits, which prevent you from making too many requests in a short time. If you exceed them, the server may block you temporarily. Finally, always handle errors gracefully; network issues or invalid inputs can cause requests to fail. By managing these aspects carefully, you can build reliable applications that interact with external services smoothly.
